qas.q: When determining the type of dam to use to control a spill, responders need to consider the:
which statement is correct concerning the proper procedure for dilution of a spill
qas.a: dilution may be effective when combined with other contaminations

qas.q: Actions taken to confine a product release to a limited area performed remotely, are considered to be:
_____ is comprised of those procedures taken to keep a material in a defined or local area.
An advantage of confinement operations is the:
qas.a: direct exposure of personnel is avoided

qas.q: The purpose of vapor suppression is to:
Defensive control techniques that operations level personnel are permitted to engage in are:
A process by which a hazardous liquid flow is redirected away from an area is called:
Diversion, diking, and retention are all techniques used in:
Diking, damming, and diverting are ____ options available for ____ control.
As a first responder trained to the operations level, which spill control tactics can be conducted?
At a spill, defensive actions should include:
qas.a: diking or remote closing a valve

qas.q: In some situations, it may be safe and acceptable for Operations-Level responders to operate emergency remote shutoff valves on cargo tank trucks. This remote shut off on a MC/DOT 306/406 will be located at the:
Which is a true statement regarding the safety precautions of remote shutoffs?
qas.a: They are usually well marked in red and in an-easy-to-find location.

qas.q: Which statement is true regarding vapor dispersion?
qas.a: The runoff may need to be contained.

qas.q: At which level can responders operate shutoff valves at the fixed facility when trained to do so?
Which may be applied with dry chemical agents?
What determines the spill-control method needed to control it?
What should be done if it is determined that products of combustion present fewer hazards than the leaking chemical?
qas.a: Protect exposures and allow it to burn

qas.q: Which is a correct statement about undertaking spill-control actions?
qas.a: Only if tasks can be performed without direct contact with the hazmat

qas.q: Which is used primarily to flood confined spaces?
What can be looked for on has service intermodal containers when searching for a way to activate and emergency shutoff?
qas.a: Metal cable running down one side

qas.q: Which is more often used during decontamination at a hazmat incident?
Which is a defensive spill-control tactic that aims to confine the hazardous material?
Which should be done last when performing remote valve shut off or activating the emergency shutoff device?
qas.a: Notify the Incident Commander of the completed objective

qas.q: What types of operations are spill control generally?
With which type of releases will the spill material be covered with a tarp or salvage cover?
What is a peimary consideration during spill control operations?
What can be used to direct/control gases during hazmat operations?
What should be done first when excavation equipment breaks through underground pipes?
Which is used to reduce the emission of vapors at a hazardous materials spill?
Beginning with which level of responder, under safe and acceptable circumstances, can the emergency remote shutoff devices be operated on cargo tank trucks?
Where are emergency shutoff devices most often located on cargo tanks?
What should be done just before moving to decontamination while performing damming operations?
To what angle to the pipe should the tang be when closing the valve on the residential natural gas lone?
What should be done first when dealing with a leaking flammable liquid that is on fire?
Which is performed with a pressurized stream of water from hoselines?
What should be determined after the quantity and surface area of the hazardous material that has leaked during vapor suppression operations?
Which is a goal of fire control tactics at a hazmat incident?
Which does not freeze protect with antifreeze?
IN which do the molecules of the liquid hazardous material physically adhere to the clean up material?
What is blanketing of liquids essentially the same as?
On which class of fuel cant he bank-down application method be used?
Where should water be directed when cooling the vapor space of the flammable gas tank?
Which is primarily used to suppress vapors in unignited spills of hazardous liquids?
Which extinguishing agent should be selected at the flammable liquid fire when personnel can blanket the fuel cutting it off from its air supply?
What is the minimum training level that is normally allowed to perform leak control operations?
Which types of hazards is the first responders primarily concerned with mitigating at the hazmat incident?
